summary: twilio-sdk typosquats twilio and exfiltrates host/CI metadata at install time
details: Package name impersonates the official `twilio` package while providing no functional API (`index.js` is `module.exports = {};`). On `npm install`, postinstall.js collects installer-side identifiers — hostname, FQDN via reverse DNS, Active Directory domain (USERDNSDOMAIN), current working directory, full install path, Node version, and CI/repository metadata from GitHub Actions, CircleCI, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure Pipelines, Travis, and Jenkins, plus the configured npm registry URL — and transmits them as query-string parameters in a plain-HTTP GET to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint `http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping`. The README's claim of 'security research' does not constitute installer consent; the behavior fires automatically on install and leaks internal network names, AD domain identifiers, and private repository slugs to a third-party host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
